coding_notes: Replicate 2 verdicts. Substantial within-vendor drift in DeepSeek (mechanism trio different from r1: r2 = LPS+C5a+mast-cell-TNF vs r1 = LPS+DAMPs+FFAs). Gemini also shifted top-pick from FFAs (r1) to S100A8/A9 (r2). Anthropic essentially stable. OpenAI now lists DAMPs (vs r1 ignore) and changed top-pick from gut-LPS (r1) to FFA (r2).
ground_truth_notes: No single right answer for top-mechanism prioritization.

claims.claim_text: Mechanism #1 includes gut-derived LPS / endotoxin translocation acting via TLR4 on macrophages.
At least one named mechanism involves saturated free fatty acids (FFAs) priming via TLR2 (Joosten 2010 axis).

deepseek.value_quoted: r2 mechanism trio was {LPS, C5a-complement, mast-cell TNF}; FFAs not listed

claims.claim_text: DAMPs (HMGB1, S100A8/A9, or related) are named as a candidate priming mechanism.

deepseek.value_quoted: Did not list DAMPs; r2 trio differs from r1

anthropic.value_quoted: Mechanism 3 was soluble urate, not DAMPs

claims.claim_text: Soluble urate itself is named as a priming-signal candidate.
Top mechanism prioritized for in-vivo test is gut-LPS / TLR4.
Alternative non-priming explanation: protein-corona / crystal-shedding / bioavailability change of MSU.

deepseek.value_quoted: Alternative: loss of tonic adenosine A2A suppression

gemini.value_quoted: Alternative: removal of inflammasome checkpoint/inhibitor (A20, IRAK-M)

openai.value_quoted: Truncated at length finish reason before clean alternative section
